__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus datasource scraping the gpon_exporter target

options.content:
| | |
|---|---|
| **Upstream length loss** | ~0.5 dB / km on 1310 nm |
| **Downstream length loss** | ~0.3 dB / km on 1490 nm |
| **Splice loss** | ~0.1 dB / splice |
| **Connector loss** | ~0.6 dB / connector |
| **Splitter loss** | log10(split-ratio) × 10 dB |

References: [UISP: designing a GPON network](https://help.ui.com/hc/en-us/articles/115011654907-UISP-Fiber-Designing-a-GPON-Network) · [ONU registration states](https://www.telecomate.com/mtk-solution-gpon-ont-onu-registration-process-and-omci-analysis) · [ONU activation paper](https://github.com/Strykar/GPON/blob/main/docs/applsci-08-01934.pdf)

| | |
|---|---|
| **User interface** | 1× port |
| **PON connector** | SC |
| **Size** | 8.2 × 6.8 × 75 mm |
| **Weight** | < 19 g |
| **Power supply** | 3.3 V |
| **Power consumption** | < 2.5 W |
| **PON modes** | GPON / EPON adaptive |
| **Op. temp (I-temp)** | −40 °C to 85 °C |
| **Op. temp (C-temp)** | 0 °C to 70 °C |
| **Storage humidity** | 5–95% non-condensing |
| **Mode** | SFU (some HGU support: DHCP, PPPoE, NAT) |
| **Configurable** | MAC, SN, vendor ID, PVID |
| **DDMI** | supported |
| **Management** | HTTP, Telnet, SSH, TR-069 |
| **SFP MSA** | supported |
| **VLAN** | transport, switch |

| | |
|---|---|
| **Downstream wavelength** | 1490 nm |
| **Upstream wavelength** | 1310 nm |
| **GPON rates** | 2.5 Gb/s down, 1.25 Gb/s up |
| **EPON rates** | 1.25 Gb/s symmetric |
| **SFP standard** | SFP MSA (SFF-8074i) |
| **DDM** | SFF-8472 |
| **Standards** | ITU-T G.984.2 (+ Amd. 1), ITU-T G.988 (OMCI) |
| **Laser safety** | class I (IEC-60825) |
| **Compliance** | RoHS |

panels.description:
0 - 3.7 V, expects close to constant 3.3 V
Internal calibration: +/-3%
panels.title: SFP voltage
SFP RAM
panels.description: Resident memory of the collector process (RSS) in MiB.

panels.title: SFP CPU
panels.description: Collector process CPU usage rate (5m). Steady low value means the daemon is fetching normally.

Internal calibration error is +/-3C
Commercial: 0 - 70C
Industrial: -40 - 85C
panels.title: SFP (commercial) SoC temperature
Signal Tx power
panels.description: Optical Tx power in dBm. Factory range -10 to +8 dBm. Internal calibration: +/-2 dB.
Internal calibration: +/-10%
panels.title: Bias current
Signal Rx power
panels.description: Optical Rx power in dBm. Factory range -28 to -8 dBm. Internal calibration: +/-2 dB.
1=O1 Initial, 5=O5 Operation (steady state), 7=O7 Emergency Stop
panels.title: ONU state

panels.description: 1 = raised, 0 = clear. Severity ranking, worst first: LOS = fibre disconnected (catastrophic); LOF = loss of frame sync (severe); LOM = loss of PLOAM message (severe); SF = signal fail, BER above threshold (severe); SD = signal degrade, BER drift (warning); TX Too Long, TX Mismatch = configuration warnings.
panels.title: Alarms

panels.description: Rate of GPON downstream codewords (~248-byte data blocks with Reed-Solomon parity) that arrived with bit errors but were repaired by Forward Error Correction. A non-zero rate is normal on long fibre runs and just means FEC is quietly doing its job before bit slips reach upper layers.
panels.title: FEC corrected codewords/sec

panels.description: Rate of codewords with more bit errors than FEC parity can repair. These errors leak into Ethernet frames, which then fail FCS and get dropped. Non-zero here means the link is degraded enough to cause user-visible packet loss.
panels.title: FEC uncorrectable codewords/sec

panels.description: Bit Interleaved Parity (BIP-8) is a per-byte checksum over the GPON Transmission Convergence frame. A non-zero rate means raw downstream bit errors regardless of whether FEC could correct them. Tracks closely with physical-layer link condition.
panels.title: Downstream BIP errors/sec

panels.description: Downstream Ethernet frame rate. Unicast and multicast are normal user traffic. FCS errors (Frame Check Sequence, the CRC32 at the end of every Ethernet frame) should stay at zero -- non-zero means corrupted frames reaching the upper layers.
panels.title: Downstream Ethernet frames/sec

panels.description: Bandwidth Map (BWMAP) traffic. The OLT broadcasts a BWMAP downstream every 125 us to allocate upstream time slots to each ONU. Received rate should be steady; CRC errors should stay at zero.
panels.title: Downstream BWMAP/sec

panels.description: Physical Layer Operations, Administration and Maintenance (PLOAM) messages. Out-of-band management traffic between OLT and ONU. Steady low rate is normal; spikes accompany ranging, key exchange, or configuration churn.
panels.title: PLOAM messages/sec

panels.description: Rate of GPON Encapsulation Method (GEM) frames carrying user payload (non-idle frames). GEM is the transport layer that wraps Ethernet for GPON. The matching idle counter saturates at 2^32-1 on this firmware so we chart non-idle alone as the link-utilisation indicator.
panels.title: Downstream GEM frames/sec

panels.description: OMCI (ONU Management and Control Interface, ITU-T G.988) admin traffic. Low steady rate is normal; spikes accompany config changes, MIB updates, or OLT-driven re-provisioning. Upstream OMCI counters are flat on this firmware so they are not charted.
panels.title: OMCI messages/sec

panels.description: Combined SN-request and ranging-request events the SFP has received in the last hour. Zero in healthy steady-state operation. Any non-zero reading means the OLT is re-ranging or re-discovering this ONU: link flap, OLT restart, or scheduled provisioning.
panels.title: Activation events (last 1h)

panels.description: Optical Tx power at the SFP, in dBm. Factory operating range -10 to +8 dBm. Internal calibration: +/-2 dB. Green inside the safe band, orange at the 1 dB edge, red outside factory range. Tx light is at 1310 nm (upstream).
panels.title: Laser Tx (1310 nm)

panels.description: Optical Rx power at the SFP, in dBm. Factory operating range -28 to -8 dBm. Internal calibration: +/-2 dB. Drift toward red is the cue to call your ISP. Rx light is at 1490 nm (downstream).
panels.title: Laser Rx (1490 nm)

panels.description: SFP SoC temperature in degrees Celsius. Internal calibration: +/-3 C. Commercial-temp parts run 0-70 C, industrial -40 to +85 C. Green inside the safe band, orange approaching the upper limit, red above 70 C (laser damage risk on commercial parts).
panels.title: SoC temperature
LAN throughput (eth0)
panels.description: Bytes/sec on the SFP's LAN-side electrical interface. This is your pass-through traffic -- everything between your host (router/switch) and the OLT crosses eth0.

panels.title: LAN errors and drops (eth0)
panels.description: Cable/PHY-layer error and drop rates on eth0 (the SFP cage contact). Should be flat zero -- non-zero means bad SFP cage seating, dirty contacts, EMI, or duplex mismatch.

panels.title: Network traffic by packets
panels.description: Packets/sec per kernel interface, RX above zero / TX below zero. eth0 and br0 carry pass-through traffic. The PON-side virtuals (pon0, eth0.2, eth0.3, nas0) are placeholder interfaces and stay at zero on this firmware.

panels.title: Conntrack table fill
panels.description: SFP-side netfilter conntrack table fill. The SFP only NATs in HGU mode -- in SFU mode (the typical setup) traffic bridges transparently and this stays near zero. This is the SFP's own conntrack, not your router's.

panels.title: TCP retransmit rate
panels.description: TCP retransmit percentage on the SFP itself, NOT your pass-through TCP traffic. The SFP only runs TCP for its own management plane (exporter's SSH every 5 min, web UI, telnet). Useful for diagnosing the management link to the SFP, not the quality of your user network.

panels.title: TCP connection events
panels.description: TCP connection events on the SFP's own stack -- mostly the exporter's SSH session opening every 5 min (passive opens). Not your client/server connections, which bridge transparently and never touch the SFP's TCP stack.

panels.title: UDP errors
panels.description: UDP error rates on the SFP's local stack. The SFP doesn't expose UDP services so these should normally be zero. Non-zero suggests a wedged daemon, port-scan from a host on the same segment, or a corrupted upstream link.

panels.title: IGMP groups joined
panels.description: Multicast groups the SFP's own kernel has joined per interface. In SFU mode every interface shows 1 (just the default 224.0.0.1 all-hosts group). Becomes interesting only on HGU/IGMP-snooping firmware where IPTV joins/leaves change the count.

panels.title: Sockets in use
panels.description: TCP/UDP socket counts on the SFP itself. TCP inuse should track the exporter SSH (1) plus a couple of internal daemons; spikes in TIME_WAIT mean an internal daemon is opening and closing connections aggressively. Not your client/server socket counts.
1 = last fetch succeeded, 0 = failed
panels.title: Collector status
SFP MAC
panels.description: Ethernet (eth0) MAC. GPON has no L2 addresses; the OLT identifies ONUs by GPON serial number.

panels.title: SFP serial
panels.description: GPON serial number assigned at manufacture. The OLT uses this to authenticate the ONU.
Firmware version reported by /etc/version on the SFP
panels.title: Firmware

panels.description: Wall time since the last successful fetch for this device
panels.title: Last fetch

panels.description: Wall-clock seconds for the last fetch. Climbing values usually mean a degraded SFP or link.
panels.title: Fetch duration

panels.description: 15m rate of fetch failures. Sustained non-zero means the collector cannot keep up with the device.
panels.title: Fetch failure rate

panels.description: How long the collector process has been running since its last restart. Pair with the "Last fetch" panel: long uptime + recent fetch = healthy; short uptime = crash/deploy; long uptime + stale last-fetch = daemon stuck.
panels.title: Daemon uptime
